articles.summary: Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond targeted ads. "Businesses often share information about people's activity on their sites with us to make ads more relevant," Meta said in a statement. "We already use this data - like games you play

keypoints: Meta expands data usage beyond ads to AI personalization
Business activity data now powers feed and chatbot responses

analysis.title: Scope expansion beyond advertising
analysis.desc: Meta leverages third-party business data previously limited to ad targeting for broader personalization purposes including AI systems.

analysis.title: Data collection transparency gaps
analysis.desc: Users may not realize activity data shared with Meta for ads now influences AI recommendations and content feeds.

analysis.title: Privacy implications for users
analysis.desc: Expanded data usage increases personal information exposure across Meta's ecosystem without explicit per-use consent mechanisms.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Meta Expands Data Usage From Ads To AI Personalization
ai_summary.bottom_line: Meta's now mining your cross-site behavior to power its AI — and you probably didn't opt into this expansion.
ai_summary.action_today: Meta → Settings & Privacy → Apps and Websites → Review business partner data sharing permissions

articles.title: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE Flaw Lets Domain Users Run Remote Code
articles.summary: Veeam has released security patches to address a critical flaw in its Backup & Replication software that could result in remote code execution. Tracked as CVE-2026-44963, the vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.4 out of a maximum of 10.0. "A vulnerability allowing remote code execution (RCE) on the Backup Server by an authenticated domain user," Veeam said in a Tuesday advisory. It

keypoints: Veeam Backup critical RCE vulnerability CVSS 9.4
Authenticated domain users can execute remote code

analysis.title: Critical severity threat
analysis.desc: CVE-2026-44963 scores 9.4 CVSS, enabling remote code execution on backup servers via authenticated access.

analysis.title: Domain user exploitation
analysis.desc: Any authenticated domain user can trigger RCE, expanding attack surface beyond external threat actors significantly.

analysis.title: Patch deployment urgent
analysis.desc: Veeam released patches. Organizations must prioritize updates immediately to prevent backup infrastructure compromise.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Critical Veeam RCE Flaw Exploitable by Domain Users
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your backup server is a target. Patch now or lose everything.
ai_summary.action_today: Veeam console → Check for updates → Install security patch immediately

articles.title: Microsoft Restores Some GitHub Repos, Keeps Others Offline as Miasma Probe Continues
articles.summary: Microsoft on Monday confirmed that it temporarily removed some GitHub repositories in response to a recent security incident that led to 73 of its open-source projects being compromised to inject an information stealer into the code. "Our priority is to protect customers and the broader ecosystem," a Microsoft spokesperson told The Hacker News via email. "We temporarily removed some

keypoints: 73 Microsoft open-source projects compromised with information stealer
GitHub repositories temporarily removed during Miasma incident investigation

analysis.title: Supply chain attack scope
analysis.desc: Microsoft's 73 compromised repositories represent significant open-source ecosystem risk affecting downstream developers and users relying on these projects.

analysis.title: Selective repository restoration
analysis.desc: Microsoft's decision to keep some repos offline suggests ongoing investigation into infection vectors and potential persistence mechanisms in vulnerable code.

analysis.title: Information stealer distribution
analysis.desc: Injected malware designed to harvest sensitive data poses immediate threat to developers who pulled compromised versions before detection and removal.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Microsoft GitHub Incident: 73 Repos Infected With Malware
ai_summary.bottom_line: If you use Microsoft open-source projects, you may have already pulled compromised code containing data-stealing malware.
